Prior to the start of the series, the mathematical formula for interdimensional portal travel was independently invented by two Ricks, namely Rick Prime and Rick C-137. They later shared this technology with other Ricks across the multiverse, who then utilized this ability to explore a defined portion of the multiverse colloquially referred to as the Central Finite Curve. Some organizations and people are aware of the existence of interdimensional teleportation, but it is unclear whether they possess the necessary understanding to employ use of it or not (e.g. Time Court, Morty Smith (304-X), and the gaseous beings of the "Fart" dimension).

name: NX-5 Planet Remover
description_source_text: The NX-5 Planet Remover is a type of ship created by the Galactic Federation that has giant laser cannons that can destroy planets. It first appears in "Star Mort Rickturn of the Jerri"

name: Gaia
description_source_text: Gaia is a sentient planet that Rick thought he impregnated.

name: Defiance's Ship
description_source_text:
The Defiance is a rebellious group opposing The New Galactic Federation, and was apparently founded and led by Beth Smith. They have launched several attacks on the New Federation in their attempts to stop its growth and eventual takeover of the galaxy.

The Defiance was founded in response to the rise of the "New Galactic Federation": a new version of the old Galactic Federation, which collapsed due to the actions of Rick Sanchez. Beth Smith formed the group with other aliens that wished to oppose the rising federation, and her actions with the group eventually made her the most wanted creature in the galaxy.

Beth led a team of resistance fighters in an assault at a New Galactic Federation outpost. They triggered explosives inside of the main building before escaping to their ship and shooting down all Gromflomite soldiers chasing them. Beth was allowing a single federation soldier to survive in order to spread the word of the Defiance. The bug soldier was, however, unable to remember her name and thereby became useless, resulting in Beth killing him as well. The team returned to their base where their doctor fixed Beth's robotic arm and found an electronic device in her neck. This revelation prompted Beth to leave the Defiance to chase down her father.

In the episode "Rickfending Your Mort", the Defiance is seen again with Birdperson joining in the interim for a mission briefing as he introduces their target Dichotomous Daniel. However, Space Beth is heard farting but she lies out it to the other members and blames it on her chair.

In "Valkyrick", the Defiance is seen arguing about their plans of assassinating the Gromflomite Queen while Space Beth skips the meeting to go to her ship, revealing that someone else has killed her beforehand. After Rick becomes involved in Space Beth's mission and berates the Defiance for her treatment, Space Beth is then fired from their team. Later on, Space Beth is presumably reinstated in the Defiance after saving the galaxy from Fantabulous' plan of transforming Gromflomites into Ultromflomites.

The Defiance (C-131)

name: Defiance's Base
description_source_text:

name: The Ocean
description_source_text:
Mr. Nimbus is a character who first appeared in "Mort Dinner Rick Andre", where he served as the episode's main antagonist.

He is the king of the ocean and Atlantis and is Rick Sanchez's nemesis, stated as such by himself as well as the latter. Mr. Nimbus lives up to this title, as he appears to be one of few beings capable of standing toe-to-toe with Rick.

Mr. Nimbus (C-131)

name: Narnia Dimension
description_source_text:
The Narnia Dimension was a pocket dimension where time moved significantly faster relative to Dimension C-131. As the speed at which time passed in the Narnia Dimension relative to Dimension C-131 isn't shown to be consistent during it's only appearance, exactly how much faster time passed within it cannot be accurately calculated (sometimes a few decades pass in half a minute and even one time, several centuries pass in less than 10 seconds).

At some point, Rick Sanchez discovered the Dimension and built a standardized portal gun that would not only access it alone, but also sync the timelines of both his universe and the Narnia Dimension as long as the portal remained open. He used it whenever he needed to rapidly age objects such as wine. Eventually an intelligent species of bovine/dog like humanoids evolved on the planet of the Dimension where the portal led, as well as horned cetaceans with bovine features as well as long-lived birds and leeches.

The planet was habitable, largely resembling Earth save for having two moons. For the most part, the species was a peaceful species that interacted friendly with interdimensional travelers. Since Rick cared little about the Dimension other than its utility to age objects, he had virtually no contact with the beings.

In an effort to distract Mr. Nimbus during a dinner/treaty signing ceremony, Rick concocted a scheme to age a set of crummy wine to superior standards. He and Morty merely tossed the wine into the said Dimension, and Rick explained the gist of the realm to Morty.

Morty gave it the name, Narnia, as its relative passage of time compared to Earth was similar to the Kingdom of Narnia in the well known children's novel series. Morty's first entrance to the Dimension to retrieve some of the wine showed the cow/dog species had reached something equivalent to the Middle Ages on Earth (hence Narnia). Morty's first interaction with the species was very positive, as a local farmer named Hoovy offered to help him move the mysterious wine that had sat near his farm for decades.

However, Morty made a critical mistake by briefly inviting Hoovy back to Earth and closing the portal behind him. As a result, several decades passed in the Narnia Dimension while only around thirty-five seconds past on Earth, ironically with Morty establishing a friendship with Hoovy and Hoovy giving Morty advice on finding love with Jessica. Hoovy arrived home to unexpectedly find his fields barren and his house dilapidated. His wife Bova, who had been pregnant with their son at his departure, had since passed away from a broken heart. His son Japheth, who had been born shortly after his departure and now a young man, stabbed his father in a fit of rage, assuming he had abandoned them. Before his death, Hoovy attested that his departure was not his choice, but rather the fault of Morty. As a result, Japheth then swore to turn his vengeance on Morty.

Morty returned a few decades later (only a minute from his perspective) to retrieve more wine, which was now conveniently hidden in a bush. Japheth, mourning his Mother and Father's grave, attacked Morty after waiting to avenge his parents' deaths. Morty was able to fend him off and escape back to his reality. He immediately returned to Narnia with a vengeance, fed up with Japheth and unwilling to let his relationship with Jessica fail. By now, Japheth was an elderly man, having restored his Father's farm and house and having three sons.

Despite Japheth's attempts to stab him, he only managed to scrape his lower left arm. As a result, Morty fatally kicked him in the rib cage and retrieved more wine before escaping. As Japheth died, his sons (who previously did not believe him about Morty) apologized to Japheth for not believing him about Morty and saying they were wrong. Japheth's last wish before he died was for his sons to kill and stop Morty at all costs. His sons swore a blood oath to avenge his death, knowing Morty would return.

Over the decades, Japheth's sons amassed great wealth and power in the medieval lands they called home. They built a massive fortress around the doorway and guarded it around the clock with an excessive amount of guards and weapons. Morty returned again, only to escape immediately, realizing the rage that had been built up against him. Japheth's sons, now older kings, believed they had won, but remained on guard in case Morty ever returned. Morty, enraged that the Narnia Dimension was blocking his evening with Jessica, spent several seconds arming himself with Rick's advanced weapons in a rage.

During this time, at least another decade passed within Narnia. One of the Kings' young sons, having witnessed Morty's defeat, assumed Morty wasn't coming back. He yelled at his Father and Uncles for wasting their kingdom's manpower and resources on the off chance of getting revenge. For his insolence, the boy was banished from the Royal Court. He was then approached and mentored for ten years by a sorcerer who

name: Elemental Rings
description_source_text:
The Elemental Rings are four magical rings that can grant the wearer(s) to manipulate the four elements: fire, water, air, and dirt.

They were owned by the Tina-Teers, but were eventually claimed by Morty, who then used the rings to kill each one of the members so that he and Planetina can be together forever.

name: Morglutz
description_source_text: Morglutz is a planet that appears in the episode "A Rickconvenient Mort." The residents of the world are Hrinchs. The planet was destroyed due to its sun consuming the planet.

name: Ferkus 9
description_source_text: Ferkus 9 is a planet that appears in the episode "A Rickconvenient Mort." Originally it was going to be destroyed by an oncoming meteor strike until the meteor was destroyed thanks to Summer's intervention.

name: Morty
description_source_text:
Mortimer "Morty" Smith Sr. of the Prime Dimension is one of the two titular main protagonists (alongside Rick Sanchez) of Rick and Morty. He was often incorrectly identified as Morty C-137 until Season 5.

Morty is Rick Sanchez's grandson and is often forced to tag along on his various misadventures as a sidekick whilst attending Harry Herpson High School along with his sister, Summer. At the beginning of the series, Morty was shown to be an incredibly anxious and submissive teenage boy. He had little say in the adventures he went on with Rick and was often verbally abused and manipulated by him. However, Morty eventually gained more confidence as time went on and is now more capable and independent rather than a mere sidekick. This newfound confidence also allowed Morty to distance himself from Rick more often due to his despicable tendencies, although Morty still stays by his side when necessary.

It has since been revealed in "Rickmurai Jack" and "Solaricks" that Rick (C-137) is not his true grandfather, as protagonist Morty is actually the biological grandson of Rick Prime, a version of Rick who murdered C-137's family. After decades of failing to get revenge on Rick Prime, Rick eventually arrived in Morty's dimension to take his place , kickstarting the series and thus Rick and Morty's adventures.

name: Space
description_source_text: Jerry Smith, commonly dubbed "Space Jerry", is the main antagonist of comic "Rick and Morty vs. the Universe: Beth 'Til Death 1". He is a clone of titular Jerry Smith and the owner of the Planet Pluto Casino.

name: Hell
description_source_text:
is a dimension home to The Devil and masochistic Hellraisers.

Rick, Jerry and Beth kidnap a demon and restrain it to a wheelchair with a turret on top. Rick explains the turret shoots shrapnel that will convert the pleasure from pain back into pain, but needs Jerry's misery to power it. When Jerry refuses, Rick sincerely apologizes for misleading Jerry and admits that he does care about him, which ends up powering the turret. They use the turret to defeat the demons and portal out of Hell. Rick aggressively tells Jerry that guy's nights are over, which he takes in stride, to Rick's annoyance.

name: Space Tahoe
description_source_text: Changeformer Bro Morty is a Rock-type Morty from the game Pocket Mortys. He got added alongside Hell Demon Morty after the premiere of Amortycan Grickfitti. He can't evolve.

name: France
description_source_text: Christina LaCroix is a very popular girl at Harry Herpson High School in the Dimension C-131.

name: Birdperson's Consciousness
description_source_text:
Senthol Diempathate is an illicit, psychoactive, and mind-altering substance of unknown origin, whose name loosely translates to "synthetic empathy." It was first referenced in season 5, episode 8, "Rickternal Friendship of the Spotless Mort." The episodes premise was a memory "flashback" where Rick and Memory Rick attempt to save Birdperson s conscious mind, so he can awaken from a coma. Various individuals, including Memory Rick and Memory Bird Person, are seen partaking in it's effects during an alien music festival.

Memory Rick is initially approached by Birdperson where he inquires about the "freely distributed, grossly illegal, Federation hallucinogen," which was rumored as to being openly shared amongst the crowd. This prompts Memory Rick to admit that he "didn't pay for it", and therefore doesn't know how much to charge in exchange for each hit. The past version of Rick is then seen offering some to Birdperson where they subsequently introduce themselves.

The effects of this drug on the user are unclear, however its names suggests that it induces a state of commutal understanding of its users emotions. Later in the episode when Rick and Birdperson are reminiscing the occurrence of their first meeting, Birdperson excitedly recalls the event as being "A great time," which points to its effects being euphoria and an uplifting psychological state. The smoke itself gives off a neon fuchsia glow and is accompanied by a high-pitched cosmic chime when exhaled. The visual representation of senthol diempathate is shown in the episode as being in a semi-gaseous form and depicted as emanating from a small vivid reddish-pink "creature" or sprite contained within a sealed vessel.

It appears that Rick (even before experiencing complications with the Best Friend Rejuvenation Sequence) had some amount of the substance in glass containers hidden in a secret compartment behind his corkboard in the garage. It is unknown whether he had previously been using it for recreational purposes, or if he had recently acquired it specifically as a contingency method of restoring Birdperson's fleeting consciousness, should any problems had arisen. This method allowed Rick to get his bearings in Birdperson's mind by triggering a state-dependent memory retrieval with the added benefit of simultaneously providing an exit route to a shared memory to later egress back into the real world.

name: Rick's Consciousness
description_source_text: "The Rickshank Rickdemption" is the first episode of the third season of Rick and Morty. It is the 22nd episode of the series overall. It premiered on April 1, 2017. It was written by Mike McMahan and directed by Juan Meza-León. The episode is rated TV-14-DLV.

name: Avian Planet
description_source_text:
Bird World is the planet where Birdperson lives. TV-signals from Earth take 20 years to reach this planet, meaning it's 20 light-years away from Earth.

Birdperson lived through the deep casualties of the "Bird Wars" in his early life, which gave way to his acute understanding of mortality and pain.

The Gromflomites and the Galactic Federation invaded Bird World during Birdperson's youth. The Galactic Federation kept the native population oppressed during their rule over the planet; a rule that ended following the Battle of Blood Ridge, where the Galactic Federation suffered a great loss. The planet returned to a more normal state where Bird People could live without fear of Gromflomites.

Birdperson made mention of his homeplanet during his visit to the Smith Residence in "Ricksy Business", and received a visit from Morty Smith in "Get Schwifty". Parts of Bird World was shown as memories during Rick's trip through Birdperson's mind in "Rickternal Friendshine of the Spotless Mort".

name: Normal Size Bug Dimension
description_source_text: The following is a list of Pocket Mortys dimensions available in the game's multiplayer mode.

name: Slartivart
description_source_text: Slartivart is a planet that appears in the episode "A Rickconvenient Mort." The residents of the world are called Broghs. The planet was destroyed when it was sucked into a black hole.

name: Rick and Two Crows Planet
description_source_text: Two Crows are Rick's replacement sidekicks from the episode Forgetting Sarick Mortshall. In the beginning of the following episode, Rickmurai Jack, Rick has left Earth to adventure with them.

name: Rick's Memories
description_source_text:
Memory Rick is a memory representation of a younger version of Rick C-137 from Birdperson C-131's mind.

After encountering the real Rick, he accompanies him in his quest to retrieve Birdperson from his subconscious. During a battle with one of his memories of Tammy, he seemingly sacrifices himself to allow Rick and Birdperson to escape his consciousness to the real world.

At the end of the episode, Memory Rick is revealed to have survived and transferred himself over to Rick's memories instead, living inside his child self's closet. However, the real Rick remembers him and travels into his own memories to find him. He is not upset that his younger counterpart opted to find a way to continue his existence and offers to make him real to share a beer. Memory Rick follows, but decides not to become real after he realizes that doing so would mean he eventually becomes present Rick.

Memory Rick returns in the episode "The Jerrick Trap". After Jerry and Rick's minds get mixed together after an accident, Memory Rick helps save both of their consciouses in an unseen scenario described as "a whole action movie". The final separation of Rick and Jerry left Memory Rick stranded in Jerry's mind. His attempts to escape are fraught as Jerry's mind seems to believe all technology is made of springs and gears, thus not allowing Memory Rick to build anything to escape. Stuck in this world, he remains tormented by Memory Gene, and has taken up making sculptures from the salvaged gears.

By “Hot Rick”, Memory Rick has been able to make workable inventions out of the gears and springs, even create a workable car out of them until he was discovered by real Rick. Memory Rick began enjoying life in Jerry's mind, being very supportive of the family. This changed Jerry's memories of him, causing Jerry to remember Rick fondly, which drew the attention of Rick. Memory Rick was transferred into Rick's desktop-like 'ant farm brain', in a digital landscape with a futuristic house. This house consisted of parts that were advanced enough for Memory Rick to escape, and hopped into Beth's mind. After learning that real Rick has separated his memory of Diane from his mind, Memory Rick manipulates Beth into going into the subbasement and allowing him access to real Rick's transferred memory of Diane. Memory Rick does this by inserting himself into Beth's memories and brainwashing her into thinking that this is how they stay a family, while giving her skills necessary to complete this. After Beth connects her brain to the ant farm brain containing the memory of Diane, Memory Rick travels to that memory, leading to Memory Rick and Memory Diane lovingly embracing before moving back into Beth’s brain. Memory Rick leaves to find out why Beth is taking so long to return to Memory Rick's ant farm brain, discovering that she has gone rogue, then comes back to find that Memory Diane has 'awoken' one of Beth's memories of her past self, leading to an outbreak of Memory Beths gaining awareness of the fact that they are memories, who all try to kill Memory Rick.

The couple goes to Beth’s recent memories to try to talk to her in real time, but are stopped by multiple memory Beths who have become sentient and desire to kill the two. The duo manage to escape into Beth’s car as she flees after killing Space Beth. Beth then decides to drive a car off a cliff against the wishes of her memory parents, but is stopped by Rick and a resuscitated Space Beth. As Beth tearfully breaks down at her conflicting memories, Memory Rick and Diane are captured by the memory Beths, but before they are killed, Real Rick gives a heartfelt speech to the real Beth, which successfully distracts all the Memory Beths long enough for Rick to sedate the real Beth. After Beth's memories are corrected, the real Rick tells the family that Memory Rick and Diane are gone. In reality, Rick built memory Rick and Diane their own independent ant farm where they can be together forever and shoots it into space, warning the duo that he will explode it if they ever try to leave. The two accept this arrangement, content to have found each other.
